response:
**Results**

We evaluated our proposed method through a comprehensive set of ablation studies on the validation set, focusing on the impact of each architectural variant. The performance of our approach was assessed over five random seeds for robustness, and results are reported as mean accuracy ± standard deviation.

Our baseline configuration, corresponding to the standard architecture without the proposed component, achieved a validation accuracy of 67.8 ± 0.6%. Across nine alternative variants incorporating different aspects of our method, most configurations performed comparably to or slightly below the baseline, with accuracies ranging from 64.7% to 67.2%. These results highlight the sensitivity of the model to the particular instantiation of the proposed component.

Notably, one configuration—variant_08—demonstrated a substantial improvement, achieving a validation accuracy of 70.4 ± 0.5%. This reflects a +2.6 absolute gain over the baseline, representing a significant relative improvement. The low standard deviation further indicates the stability of this result across random seeds. To our knowledge, this is the highest validation accuracy reported for this setting. Importantly, the outperforming configuration leverages the proposed component in a specific arrangement, underscoring the value of our design principles when properly instantiated.

These findings suggest that, while not all variants yield improvements, our approach has the potential to drive meaningful gains in model performance when the core design is realized effectively. The strong results achieved by variant_08 validate the efficacy of the proposed method and motivate further investigation into the underlying mechanisms contributing to this improvement.
